# ShutterCount

> Free camera shutter count checker. Drop a RAW photo file and instantly read the shutter actuations directly in your browser — no server upload, no account required. Supports Canon CR3/CR2, Nikon NEF, Sony ARW, Fujifilm RAF, Olympus ORF, Panasonic RW2, Adobe DNG, and JPEG/JPG. Validated on 210+ camera models.

ShutterCount is a static, client-side web application hosted at shuttercount.app. All RAW file parsing happens entirely in the browser using JavaScript. No file content ever leaves the user's device. The shutter count is extracted from the EXIF MakerNote data embedded in the RAW file.

## Main Application

- [ShutterCount — Free Camera Shutter Count Checker](https://shuttercount.app/): The main tool. Drag and drop or select any supported RAW file to instantly display the camera's shutter count, camera model, and other EXIF metadata. Supports Canon, Nikon, Sony, Fujifilm, Olympus, Panasonic, and DNG files.
- [Supported Camera Landing Pages](https://shuttercount.app/supported-cameras/): Central directory for model-specific camera landing pages with technical notes and shutter-count guidance.


## Photo Editing Software Comparisons

- [Photo Editing Software Comparisons](https://shuttercount.app/photo-editing-software/): Pilot collection of English comparison articles positioning Zoner Studio as a practical all-in-one workflow for RAW editing, organizing, retouching, sharing, printing, and simple video work.
- [Zoner Studio vs Capture One](https://shuttercount.app/zoner-studio-vs-capture-one/): Compares Zoner Studio with Capture One for RAW development, catalog/session workflows, tethering, retouching, sharing, and everyday usability.
- [Zoner Studio vs Affinity Photo](https://shuttercount.app/zoner-studio-vs-affinity-photo/): Compares Zoner Studio with Affinity Photo for complete photo management, RAW development, layers, cataloging, batch workflow, and output.
- [Zoner Studio vs DxO PhotoLab](https://shuttercount.app/zoner-studio-vs-dxo-photolab/): Compares Zoner Studio with DxO PhotoLab for RAW processing, denoising, optical corrections, organization, retouching, and all-in-one flexibility.
- [Zoner Studio vs darktable](https://shuttercount.app/zoner-studio-vs-darktable/): Compares Zoner Studio with darktable for RAW development, usability, organization, support, output, and polished everyday workflow.

## Canon Camera Shutter Count Pages

- [Canon EOS R Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-r-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS R mirrorless camera. Explains how to read CR3 shutter count from EXIF, rated shutter life, and what the count means when buying used.
- [Canon EOS R1 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-r1-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Canon EOS R1 flagship mirrorless camera.
- [Canon EOS R3 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-r3-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS R3. Covers the electronic and mechanical shutter, rated life, and how to extract count from CR3 files.
- [Canon EOS R5 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-r5-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS R5. Includes rated mechanical shutter life (500,000 actuations), how to check via CR3 RAW file, and buying-used advice.
- [Canon EOS R6 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-r6-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Canon EOS R6, including rated shutter life and CR3 EXIF extraction.
- [Canon EOS R6 Mark II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-r6-mark-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS R6 Mark II.
- [Canon EOS R7 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-r7-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Canon EOS R7 APS-C mirrorless camera.
- [Canon EOS R8 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-r8-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS R8 entry-level full-frame mirrorless camera.
- [Canon EOS R10 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-r10-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Canon EOS R10 APS-C mirrorless camera.
- [Canon EOS R50 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-r50-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS R50 compact mirrorless camera.
- [Canon EOS R100 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-r100-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Canon EOS R100 entry-level mirrorless camera.
- [Canon EOS RP Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-rp-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS RP full-frame mirrorless camera.
- [Canon EOS 350D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-350d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 350D (Rebel XT / Kiss Digital N, 2005) — one of Canon's most iconic early consumer DSLRs with an 8 MP CMOS sensor and DIGIC II processor. CR2 consumer limitation: shutter count not stored in RAW files; must be read via USB PTP using gphoto2 (Linux/macOS) or EOSInfo (Windows). Estimated ~100,000-actuation shutter life. Covers 350D vs 400D differences, EF/EF-S lens compatibility, sensor and capacitor aging on this 20-year-old classic DSLR, and used-camera buying guidance.
- [Canon EOS 400D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-400d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 400D (Rebel XTi / Kiss X, 2006) — one of Canon's best-selling entry-level DSLRs, first Rebel with the Integrated Dust Reduction System (IDSC). 10.1 MP APS-C sensor, DIGIC II, CF card slot, 100,000-actuation rated shutter. CR2 consumer limitation: shutter count not stored in RAW files; must be read via USB PTP using gphoto2 (Linux/macOS) or EOSInfo (Windows). Covers BP-511A battery, mirror damper aging on older bodies, and used-camera buying guidance for this nearly 20-year-old classic.
- [Canon EOS 450D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-450d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 450D (Rebel XSi / Kiss X2, 2008) entry-level DSLR. First Canon Rebel with Live View and a 3-inch LCD. 12.2 MP APS-C sensor, DIGIC III, CR2 format. Rated 100,000 actuations. CR2 consumer limitation: shutter count is not stored in RAW files and must be read via USB PTP (gphoto2 on Linux/macOS, EOSInfo on Windows). Buying guidance for this 15+ year old classic entry body.
- [Canon EOS 500D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-500d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 500D (Rebel T1i / Kiss X3, 2009) entry-level DSLR. The first Canon consumer DSLR to record 1080p Full HD video. 15.1 MP APS-C sensor, DIGIC 4, CR2 format. Rated 100,000 actuations. CR2 consumer limitation applies — shutter count not in-file; requires USB PTP connection. Buying guidance including note on video-use shutter wear acceleration.
- [Canon EOS 550D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-550d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 550D (Rebel T2i / Kiss X4, 2010) entry-level DSLR. Covers the 100,000-actuation rated shutter, why CR2 consumer files don't store count in-file, how to check via USB using gphoto2 or EOSInfo, Magic Lantern RAW video impact on shutter wear, and buying guidance for this historically popular beginner body.
- [Canon EOS 600D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-600d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 600D (Rebel T3i / Kiss X5, 2011) entry-level DSLR. Covers the 100,000-actuation rated shutter, CR2 consumer in-file limitation (count not embedded), USB PTP extraction methods (gphoto2, EOSInfo, ShutterCheck), the fully articulating Vari-angle LCD, and what to check on these 10+ year old bodies when buying used.
- [Canon EOS 100D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-100d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 100D (Rebel SL1 / Kiss X7, 2013) — the world's smallest and lightest DSLR at launch. 18 MP APS-C sensor, Hybrid CMOS AF II, DIGIC 5, CR2 format. Estimated ~100,000 actuations. Covers the camera menu limitation (count not displayed), how to read via CR2 MakerNote with shuttercount.app, and buying guidance for this compact 10+ year old travel DSLR.
- [Canon EOS 1100D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-1100d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 1100D (Rebel T3 / Kiss X50, 2011) — one of Canon's most popular entry-level DSLRs, widely sold as a first DSLR. 12.1 MP APS-C sensor, DIGIC 4, 720p video, LP-E10 battery, 100,000-actuation rated shutter. CR2 consumer limitation: shutter count not stored in RAW files; must be read via USB PTP using gphoto2 (Linux/macOS) or EOSInfo (Windows). Covers beginner use patterns (many units have low shutter counts), LP-E10 battery availability, and used-camera buying guidance for this globally popular starter DSLR.
- [Canon EOS 1200D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-1200d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 1200D (Rebel T5 / Kiss X70, 2014) entry-level DSLR. One of Canon's best-selling beginner DSLRs globally. 18 MP APS-C sensor, DIGIC 4, CR2 format. Estimated ~100,000 actuations. Covers how to read the shutter count from CR2 RAW via shuttercount.app, comparison with the 1300D successor, and buying guidance for this widely available used camera.
- [Canon EOS 1300D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-1300d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 1300D (Rebel T6 / Kiss X80, 2016) entry-level DSLR. One of Canon's best-selling beginner DSLRs with built-in Wi-Fi and NFC. 18 MP APS-C sensor, DIGIC 4+, CR2 format. Rated 100,000 actuations. CR2 consumer limitation: shutter count not stored in-file; requires USB PTP (gphoto2, EOSInfo). Comparison with the 1200D and 2000D successors.
- [Canon EOS 2000D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-2000d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 2000D (Rebel T7 / 1500D, 2018) entry-level DSLR. Covers the 100,000-actuation rated shutter, CR2 RAW format (DIGIC 4+), how to check via shuttercount.app, and what counts are typical when buying this popular beginner camera used.
- [Canon EOS 4000D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-4000d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 4000D (Rebel T100 / Kiss X90, 2018) — Canon's most affordable DSLR, aimed at first-time buyers. 18 MP APS-C CMOS sensor, DIGIC 4+, Wi-Fi, 2.7-inch fixed LCD, 3 fps burst, EF/EF-S mount, LP-E10 battery. CR2 consumer limitation: shutter count is not stored in RAW files. Guide covers how to estimate usage from file numbers, what a Panasonic service centre can read, typical usage patterns for entry-level buyers, and how the 4000D compares to the 2000D (24 MP, larger LCD) released simultaneously. Covers CR3-based successors (250D, 850D) that do support in-file shutter count reading.
- [Canon EOS 200D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-200d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 200D (Rebel SL2 / Kiss X9). Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations. CR2 format.
- [Canon EOS 250D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-250d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 250D (Rebel SL3). CR3 format; rated lifespan and buying guidance.
- [Canon EOS 77D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-77d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 77D. Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations. CR2 format.
- [Canon EOS 700D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-700d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 700D (Rebel T5i / Kiss X7i, 2013) entry-level DSLR. Covers the 100,000-actuation rated shutter, CR2 RAW format, articulating touchscreen, and what to check when buying this popular used camera — one of the best-selling Rebel-series bodies of its era.
- [Canon EOS 750D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-750d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 750D (Rebel T6i / Kiss X8i, 2015) — the first Canon Rebel with Dual Pixel CMOS AF. Covers the 100,000-actuation rated shutter, how to read the count from CR2 files via the Canon MakerNote IFD, the 19-point all-cross-type AF upgrade over the 700D, and what to check when buying this popular used camera.
- [Canon EOS 800D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-800d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 800D (Rebel T7i / Kiss X9i). Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations. CR2 format.
- [Canon EOS 850D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-850d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 850D (Rebel T8i). CR3 format; rated lifespan and buying guidance.
- [Canon EOS 80D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-80d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 80D. Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations. CR2 format.
- [Canon EOS 90D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-90d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 90D. Rated lifespan: 120,000 actuations. CR3 format.
- [Canon EOS 70D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-70d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 70D — the DSLR that introduced Dual Pixel AF. Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations. CR2 format.
- [Canon EOS 60D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-60d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 60D — the first xxD with a fully articulating screen. Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations. CR2 format.
- [Canon EOS 50D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-50d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 50D prosumer DSLR. Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations. CR2 format.
- [Canon EOS 40D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-40d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 40D (2007) enthusiast APS-C DSLR. Covers the 100,000-actuation rated shutter, CR2 RAW format, how to check via shuttercount.app, and what to look for when buying this classic used camera — including the importance of verifying count on older bodies that may have accumulated significant actuations.
- [Canon EOS 30D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-30d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 30D (2006) enthusiast APS-C DSLR — the direct successor to the 20D. 8.2 MP CMOS sensor, DIGIC II, 100,000-actuation rated shutter (double the 20D's 50,000). CR2 consumer limitation: shutter count not stored in RAW files; must be read via USB PTP using gphoto2 (Linux/macOS) or EOSInfo (Windows). Covers 30D vs 20D differences (3-inch LCD, multi-controller joystick), and used-camera buying guidance for this ~20-year-old DSLR.
- [Canon EOS 20D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-20d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 20D (2004) — Canon's mainstream APS-C DSLR that helped define the enthusiast DSLR category. 8.2 MP CMOS sensor, DIGIC II, 50,000-actuation rated shutter (lower rating reflecting 2004-era technology). CR2 consumer limitation: shutter count not stored in RAW files; must be read via USB PTP using gphoto2 (Linux/macOS) or EOSInfo (Windows). Covers 20D history, CF card usage, BP-511A battery, and buying guidance for this 20+ year old classic.
- [Canon EOS 7D Mark II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-7d-mark-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 7D Mark II. Rated lifespan: 200,000 actuations. CR2 format.
- [Canon EOS 7D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-7d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 7D. Rated lifespan: 150,000 actuations. CR2 format.
- [Canon EOS 6D Mark II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-6d-mark-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 6D Mark II. Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations. CR2 format.
- [Canon EOS 6D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-6d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 6D. Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations. CR2 format.
- [Canon EOS 5D Mark IV Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-5d-mark-iv-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 5D Mark IV. Rated lifespan: 300,000 actuations. CR2 format.
- [Canon EOS 5D Mark III Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-5d-mark-iii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 5D Mark III. Rated lifespan: 150,000 actuations. CR2 format.
- [Canon EOS 5D Mark II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-5d-mark-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 5D Mark II. Rated lifespan: 150,000 actuations. CR2 format.
- [Canon EOS 5DS Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-5ds-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 5DS (2015) — Canon's 50.6 MP full-frame DSLR, the highest-resolution Canon at launch. Covers the 150,000-actuation rated shutter, how to read the count from CR2 MakerNote, mirror lockup mode and its effect on the count, 5DS vs 5DS R differences (no-OLPF variant), and buying guidance for landscape and commercial photographers.
- [Canon EOS 5DS R Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-5ds-r-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 5DS R (2015) — the low-pass filter cancellation (LPF-cancel) variant of the 5DS, producing slightly sharper fine detail at the cost of marginally increased moiré risk. 50.6 MP full-frame CMOS, rated 150,000 actuations, CR2 RAW format. Shutter count readable from CR2 MakerNote CTMD block. Covers 5DS vs 5DS R differences, EF lens compatibility, the 150,000-actuation rated shutter, and buying guidance for studio and landscape photographers.
- [Canon EOS 5D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-5d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the original Canon EOS 5D. Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations. CR2 format.
- [Canon EOS M50 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-m50-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS M50. CR3 format; rated lifespan and buying guidance.
- [Canon EOS M50 Mark II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-m50-mark-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS M50 Mark II. 24.1 MP APS-C mirrorless; CR3 format.
- [Canon EOS M6 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-m6-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS M6 (2017) — Canon's compact EVF-less APS-C mirrorless with 24.2 MP Dual Pixel CMOS AF and DIGIC 7. Estimated shutter life ~100,000 actuations (not officially published). Uses CR2 RAW format (predecessor to CR3). Covers the shutter count check method and comparison with the M6 Mark II.
- [Canon EOS M6 Mark II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-m6-mark-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS M6 Mark II. 32.5 MP APS-C mirrorless; CR3 format.
- [Canon EOS M5 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-m5-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS M5 (2016) — Canon's first enthusiast EF-M mirrorless with a built-in EVF. 24.2 MP APS-C Dual Pixel CMOS AF, DIGIC 7, estimated ~100,000-actuation shutter life. Uses CR2 RAW format. Covers the shutter count check method, used-camera buying guidance, and comparison with the M6 and M6 Mark II.
- [Canon EOS M200 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-m200-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS M200 (2019) — Canon's most compact entry-level EF-M mirrorless. 24.1 MP APS-C DPAF, DIGIC 8, 4K video, no electronic shutter, estimated ~100,000 actuations. Uses CR3 RAW format.
- [Canon EOS M3 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-m3-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS M3 (2015) — Canon's enthusiast EF-M mirrorless with 24.2 MP APS-C Dual Pixel CMOS AF and a built-in flash. Rated 100,000 actuations. Uses CR2 RAW format; CR2 consumer bodies do not store shutter count in-file — requires USB PTP via EOSInfo (Windows), ShutterCount (macOS), or Canon service mode. Covers EF-M mount, EF-EOS M adapter compatibility, and comparison with the EOS M50.
- [Canon EOS M10 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-m10-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS M10 (2015) — Canon's most compact EF-M mirrorless of its era. 18 MP APS-C CMOS, DIGIC 6, tilting 3-inch touchscreen, built-in pop-up flash, Wi-Fi. Estimated ~100,000 actuations (not officially published). Uses CR2 RAW format. Particularly popular in Asian markets as a compact lifestyle camera. Covers shutter count check method, buying guidance for this decade-old body, and comparison with the M100 successor.
- [Canon EOS M100 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-m100-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS M100 (2017) — Canon's ultra-compact beginner EF-M mirrorless with 24.1 MP Dual Pixel CMOS AF and DIGIC 7. Estimated ~100,000 actuations (not officially published). Uses CR2 RAW format. Fixed 3-inch touchscreen (no tilt), Wi-Fi and Bluetooth. Covers shutter count check method, comparison with the M200 successor, and used-camera buying guidance.
- [Canon EOS R5 Mark II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-r5-mark-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS R5 Mark II. Rated lifespan: 500,000 actuations. Shutter count confirmed from CR3 CTMD at offset 0x069C.
- [Canon EOS R5 C Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-r5-c-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS R5 C (2022) — Canon's cinema hybrid combining the 45 MP R5 sensor with Cinema EOS video pipeline, active cooling, 8K RAW internal recording, and USB-PD power. Rated shutter life: 500,000 actuations. CR3 format. Note: video recording uses electronic shutter and does not increment the mechanical counter.
- [Canon EOS R6 Mark III Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-r6-mark-iii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS R6 Mark III. Shutter count confirmed from CR3 CTMD at offset 0x086D.
- [Canon EOS R50 V Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-r50-v-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS R50 V. CR3 CTMD offset 0x086D.
- [Canon EOS 1D X Mark III Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-1dx-mark-iii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS-1D X Mark III flagship. Rated lifespan: 500,000 actuations. CR3 format.
- [Canon EOS 1D X Mark II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-1dx-mark-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS-1D X Mark II. Rated lifespan: 400,000 actuations. CR2 format.
- [Canon EOS 1D X Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-1dx-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS-1D X. Rated lifespan: 400,000 actuations. CR2 format.
- [Canon EOS 1D Mark IV Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-1d-mark-iv-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS-1D Mark IV. Rated lifespan: 300,000 actuations. CR2 format; shutter count available from in-file MakerNote.
- [Canon EOS 1D Mark III Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-1d-mark-iii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS-1D Mark III. Rated lifespan: 300,000 actuations. CR2 format; shutter count available from in-file MakerNote.
- [Canon EOS 1Ds Mark III Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-1ds-mark-iii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS-1Ds Mark III. Rated lifespan: 300,000 actuations. CR2 format; shutter count available from in-file MakerNote.
- [Canon EOS 1D Mark II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-1d-mark-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS-1D Mark II / 1D Mark II N. Rated lifespan: 200,000 actuations. CR2 format; shutter count available from in-file MakerNote.
- [Canon EOS 650D Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-650d-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Canon EOS 650D (Rebel T4i / Kiss X6i, 2012) — the first Canon DSLR with a touchscreen and Hybrid CMOS AF for continuous video AF. Covers the 100,000-actuation rated shutter, why CR2 consumer files don't store the count in-file, USB PTP extraction methods (gphoto2, EOSInfo), Magic Lantern video use impact on mechanical shutter wear, and buying guidance for this historically popular entry-level DSLR.
- [Canon EOS M Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/canon-eos-m-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the original Canon EOS M (2012) — Canon's first mirrorless interchangeable-lens camera. 18 MP APS-C CMOS sensor, hybrid CDAF/PDAF autofocus, EF-M mount with EF-to-EF-M adapter for legacy lens compatibility, Full HD 1080p video, CR2 format. Estimated shutter life ~100,000 actuations (not officially published). Shutter count readable from CR2 MakerNote via shuttercount.app. Covers the slow AF issue fixed by firmware update, EF-M vs RF mount compatibility (not interchangeable), CR2 vs later CR3 format distinction, EOS M vs EOS M50 upgrade comparison, and buying guidance for this 12-year-old entry-level mirrorless.

## Nikon Camera Shutter Count Pages

- [Nikon D500 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-d500-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon D500 APS-C DSLR. Covers rated shutter life (200,000 actuations) and NEF EXIF extraction.
- [Nikon D750 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-d750-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Nikon D750 full-frame DSLR.
- [Nikon D850 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-d850-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon D850. Includes rated shutter life (200,000 actuations) and NEF EXIF count extraction.
- [Nikon Z5 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-z5-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon Z5 entry-level full-frame mirrorless camera.
- [Nikon Z5 II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-z5-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Nikon Z5 II.
- [Nikon Z6 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-z6-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon Z6 full-frame mirrorless camera.
- [Nikon Z6 II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-z6-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Nikon Z6 II.
- [Nikon Z6 III Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-z6-iii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon Z6 III with partial stacked sensor.
- [Nikon Z7 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-z7-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Nikon Z7 high-resolution full-frame mirrorless camera.
- [Nikon Z7 II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-z7-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon Z7 II.
- [Nikon Z8 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-z8-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon Z8. Unlike the Z9, the Z8 has a mechanical shutter rated at 500,000 actuations.
- [Nikon Z9 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-z9-shutter-count/): The Nikon Z9 has no mechanical shutter — its shutter count is always 0. Explains why the Z9 shutter count reads zero from NEF files and what to assess instead when buying used.
- [Nikon Z30 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-z30-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Nikon Z30 vlog-oriented APS-C mirrorless camera.
- [Nikon Z50 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-z50-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon Z50 APS-C mirrorless camera.
- [Nikon Z50 II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-z50-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Nikon Z50 II.
- [Nikon Zf Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-zf-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon Zf retro-styled full-frame mirrorless camera.
- [Nikon Zfc Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-zfc-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Nikon Zfc retro APS-C mirrorless camera.
- [Nikon 1 V1 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-1-v1-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon 1 V1 / J1 mirrorless cameras. Reads MakerNote tag 0x00A7 from NEF files.
- [Nikon 1 V2 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-1-v2-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon 1 V2 (2012) — the second V-series flagship of the Nikon 1 system. 14.2 MP 1-inch CMOS CX sensor, Expeed 3A processor, 15 fps burst with AF tracking, 60 fps fixed-focus burst, built-in 1.44M-dot OLED EVF, integrated grip, Full HD 1080p video, EN-EL21 battery (different from the J-series EN-EL20). Estimated shutter life ~100,000 actuations (not officially published). Reads MakerNote tag 0x00A7 from NEF files — same tag across the entire Nikon 1 lineup. Covers V2 vs V1 differences (14.2 MP, 15fps, improved grip), V2 vs V3 differences (V3 removed built-in EVF in favour of modular design), FT-1 adapter compatibility, and used-camera buying guidance.
- [Nikon 1 J5 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-1-j5-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon 1 J5 (2015) — the final and highest-resolution Nikon 1 J-series camera, featuring a 20.8 MP 1-inch BSI CMOS sensor, 20 fps burst with AF tracking, 180°-tilting touchscreen, and 4K frame extraction. Estimated shutter life ~100,000 actuations (not officially published). Reads MakerNote tag 0x00A7 from NEF files — same tag used across the entire Nikon 1 lineup. Covers electronic vs mechanical shutter counter behaviour, EN-EL24 battery age considerations, discontinued Nikon 1 lens ecosystem, and used-camera buying guidance.
- [Nikon 1 J2 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-1-j2-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon 1 J2 (2012) — the refined second-generation J-series compact mirrorless with the same 10.1 MP 1-inch CMOS sensor and Expeed 3A processor as the J1, but with an improved exterior design, better grip texture, expanded color options, and improved video controls. 10 fps burst with AF tracking, 60 fps fixed-focus burst, Full HD 1080/60i video, built-in flash, EN-EL20 battery, microSD card. Estimated shutter life ~100,000 actuations (not officially published). Reads MakerNote tag 0x00A7 from NEF files. Covers J2 vs J1 differences (design, color, video controls), J2 vs J3 differences (J3 added 14.2 MP and EFCS), FT-1 adapter and 2.7× crop factor for telephoto reach, EN-EL20 battery aging, and used-camera buying guidance.
- [Nikon 1 J1 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-1-j1-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon 1 J1 (2011) — the original Nikon 1 compact system camera and launch model of the entire Nikon 1 lineup. 10.1 MP 1-inch CMOS sensor, Expeed 3A processor, 10 fps burst with hybrid phase/contrast-detect AF, 30 fps fixed-focus burst, Full HD 1080/60i video, built-in flash, EN-EL20 battery. Estimated shutter life ~100,000 actuations (not officially published). Reads MakerNote tag 0x00A7 from NEF files — same tag across the Nikon 1 series. Covers what made hybrid PDAF groundbreaking in 2011, J1 vs V1 differences (V1 had built-in EVF), Nikon 1 (CX) mount lens compatibility, EN-EL20 battery aging on 13+ year old bodies, and used-camera buying guidance.
- [Nikon 1 J3 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-1-j3-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon 1 J3 (2013) — the third-generation J-series compact mirrorless with a 14.2 MP 1-inch CMOS sensor, 15 fps burst with AF tracking, 60 fps fixed-focus burst, electronic front curtain shutter (EFCS), Full HD 1080p video, built-in flash, EN-EL20 battery. Estimated shutter life ~100,000 actuations (not officially published). Reads MakerNote tag 0x00A7 from NEF files. Covers J3 vs J1 improvements (sensor resolution, burst speed, EFCS), J3 vs J4 differences (18.4 MP, 4K frame extraction on J4), sports photography use case with Nikon 1's 2.7× crop factor, and used-camera buying guidance.
- [Nikon 1 J4 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-1-j4-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon 1 J4 (2014) — the fourth-generation J-series compact mirrorless featuring an 18.4 MP back-illuminated (BSI) 1-inch CMOS sensor, 20 fps burst with full AF tracking, and 4K photo mode via frame extraction. A significant resolution jump over the 14.2 MP J3. Estimated shutter life ~100,000 actuations (not officially published). Reads MakerNote tag 0x00A7 from NEF files — same tag used across the entire Nikon 1 lineup. Covers J4 vs J3 differences (18.4 MP BSI sensor, 4K frame extraction), note on burst-mode shutter count accumulation (20 fps means 100 shots per 5 seconds), EN-EL20a battery age considerations, and used-camera buying guidance.
- [Nikon 1 V3 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-1-v3-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon 1 V3 (2014) — the flagship and final V-series Nikon 1 body. 18.4 MP 1-inch CMOS sensor, Expeed 4A processor, 20 fps burst with AF tracking, 60 fps fixed-focus burst, 3-inch tilting 1,037k-dot touchscreen, optional DF-M1 EVF and GR-N1010 grip accessories, EN-EL20a battery. Estimated shutter life ~100,000 actuations. Reads MakerNote tag 0x00A7 from NEF files. Notable: unlike V1 and V2, the V3 has no built-in EVF — it was removed in favour of a slimmer modular design. Covers V3 vs V1 differences, optional accessory availability on the used market, electronic vs mechanical shutter counter behaviour, and buying guidance for bird/wildlife photographers attracted by the 2.7× crop factor.
- [Nikon D6 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-d6-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon D6 professional DSLR. Rated lifespan: 400,000 actuations. NEF MakerNote tag 0x00A7.
- [Nikon D5 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-d5-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon D5 professional DSLR. Rated lifespan: 400,000 actuations.
- [Nikon D4S Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-d4s-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon D4S. Rated lifespan: 400,000 actuations.
- [Nikon D4 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-d4-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon D4 professional DSLR. Rated lifespan: 400,000 actuations.
- [Nikon D3X Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-d3x-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon D3X. Reads MakerNote tag 0x00A7.
- [Nikon D3 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-d3-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon D3 flagship DSLR. Rated lifespan: 300,000 actuations.

- [Nikon D90 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-d90-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon D90 — the iconic 12.3 MP DX DSLR that introduced video to Nikon DSLRs.
- [Nikon D80 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-d80-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon D80 classic 10 MP enthusiast DSLR. Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations.
- [Nikon D70 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-d70-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon D70 / D70s — the DSLR that changed everything. Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations.
- [Nikon D70s Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-d70s-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon D70s (2005) — updated D70 with a larger 2-inch LCD, MB-D70 battery grip support (including AA batteries), and the same 6.1 MP APS-C CCD sensor. Shutter count stored in NEF MakerNote tag 0x00A7, readable from NEF files via shuttercount.app or ExifTool. Estimated ~50,000–100,000 actuation shutter life. Uses CompactFlash (CF) cards. Covers D70 vs D70s differences, CCD sensor aging considerations, and used-camera buying guidance for this 20-year-old classic.
- [Nikon D60 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-d60-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon D60 compact entry-level DSLR with sensor cleaning.
- [Nikon D50 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-d50-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon D50 — Nikon's first truly affordable DSLR.
- [Nikon D40X Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-d40x-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon D40X classic entry-level DSLR. Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations.
- [Nikon D40 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/nikon-d40-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Nikon D40 / D40X compact entry-level DSLR.

## Sony Camera Shutter Count Pages

- [Sony A1 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a1-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony Alpha 1 flagship mirrorless camera. Covers ARW EXIF extraction and the 500,000-actuation rated shutter life.
- [Sony A6400 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a6400-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Sony A6400 APS-C mirrorless camera.
- [Sony A6600 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a6600-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A6600 APS-C mirrorless camera.
- [Sony A6700 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a6700-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Sony A6700 APS-C mirrorless camera.
- [Sony A7 II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a7-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A7 II full-frame mirrorless camera.
- [Sony A7 III Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a7-iii-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Sony A7 III, including rated 200,000-actuation shutter life.
- [Sony A7 IV Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a7-iv-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A7 IV. Covers ARW shutter count extraction and rated shutter life.
- [Sony A7C Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a7c-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Sony A7C compact full-frame mirrorless camera.
- [Sony A7C II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a7c-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A7C II.
- [Sony A7CR Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a7cr-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Sony A7CR high-resolution compact full-frame camera.
- [Sony A7R III Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a7r-iii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A7R III high-resolution mirrorless camera.
- [Sony A7R IV Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a7r-iv-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Sony A7R IV 61 MP mirrorless camera.
- [Sony A7R V Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a7r-v-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A7R V flagship high-resolution mirrorless camera.
- [Sony A7S III Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a7s-iii-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Sony A7S III video-oriented full-frame mirrorless camera.
- [Sony A9 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a9-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A9 sports mirrorless camera.
- [Sony A9 II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a9-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Sony A9 II.
- [Sony A9 III Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a9-iii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A9 III with global shutter sensor. Explains the electronic shutter implications for shutter count.
- [Sony ZV-E1 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-zv-e1-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Sony ZV-E1 full-frame vlog camera.
- [Sony ZV-E10 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-zv-e10-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony ZV-E10 APS-C vlog camera.
- [Sony A5100 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a5100-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A5100 (ILCE-5100, 2014) — the world's smallest APS-C mirrorless at launch. Covers the 100,000-actuation rated shutter, ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050 (encrypted cubic residue cipher), how to check via shuttercount.app, and buying guidance for this popular compact E-mount camera.
- [Sony Alpha A77 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a77-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony Alpha A77 (SLT-A77, 2011) — Sony's flagship APS-C SLT camera with a 24.3 MP sensor, 12 fps burst with continuous AF, weather sealing, and articulating OLED EVF. Rated 150,000 actuations. ARW format; shutter count readable via encrypted MakerNote tag 0x9050. Explains SLT translucent-mirror design and how it affects shutter wear. Buying guidance for this A-mount sports and wildlife body.
- [Sony A1 II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a1-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A1 II (ILCE-1M2) flagship mirrorless camera. Shutter count readable from ARW via MakerNote tag 0x9050.
- [Sony A6000 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a6000-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A6000 — Sony's best-selling mirrorless camera. Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations. ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050.
- [Sony A6100 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a6100-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A6100 APS-C mirrorless. Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations. ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050.
- [Sony A6300 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a6300-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A6300 APS-C mirrorless. Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations. ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050.
- [Sony A6500 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a6500-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A6500 APS-C mirrorless. Rated lifespan: 200,000 actuations. ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050.
- [Sony A7 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a7-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the original Sony A7 (ILCE-7) full-frame mirrorless. Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations.
- [Sony A7R Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a7r-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the original Sony A7R 36.4 MP full-frame mirrorless. ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050.
- [Sony A7R II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a7r-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A7R II. Rated lifespan: 500,000 actuations. ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050.
- [Sony A7S Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a7s-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A7S full-frame video-oriented mirrorless. Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations.
- [Sony A7S II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a7s-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A7S II. Rated lifespan: approximately 200,000 actuations. ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050.
- [Sony A7V Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a7v-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A7V full-frame mirrorless. Rated lifespan: 500,000 actuations.
- [Sony FX3 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-fx3-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony FX3 full-frame cinema camera. Rated lifespan: 200,000 actuations. ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050.
- [Sony FX30 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-fx30-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony FX30 APS-C cinema camera with E-mount versatility. ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050.
- [Sony FX6 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-fx6-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony FX6 (ILME-FX6) full-frame BSI Cinema Line camera (2020). Covers the ~200,000-actuation estimated shutter life, ARW MakerNote encrypted tag 0x9050, the critical caveat that video recording does not use the mechanical shutter (low counts are expected and normal on cinema bodies), how to evaluate total operating hours instead of just shutter count, and buying guidance for this professional cinema camera.
- [Sony FX2 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-fx2-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony FX2 (ILME-FX2, 2023) full-frame Cinema Line camera — the highest-resolution Cinema Line body with a 33 MP BSI sensor shared with the A7C II. Covers the ~200,000-actuation estimated shutter life, ARW MakerNote encrypted tag 0x9050 (same decryption path as the A7C II, A7CR and A6700), the critical caveat that video recording does not increment the mechanical counter, and buying guidance for this compact hybrid cinema/stills body.
- [Sony ZV-E10 II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-zv-e10-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony ZV-E10 II APS-C vlog camera. ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050.
- [Sony a5000 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a5000-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony Alpha a5000 (2014) — Sony's most compact APS-C mirrorless at launch with Wi-Fi/NFC and 20.1 MP sensor. Covers the estimated ~100,000-actuation lifespan (Sony does not publish official ratings for this model), ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050, JPEG MakerNote stripping caveat, and what to look for when buying this entry-level E-mount camera used.
- [Sony Alpha A77 II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a77-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony Alpha A77 II (ILCA-77M2, 2014) — Sony's final APS-C SLT flagship with 12 fps burst and 79-point AF. Covers the 150,000-actuation rated shutter, ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050 (same encrypted structure as E-mount cameras), how SLT technology (fixed mirror) means every shot increments the mechanical counter, and buying guidance for this high-speed A-mount camera.
- [Sony Alpha 99 II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a99-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony Alpha 99 II (ILCA-99M2, 2016) — Sony's final and most capable A-mount full-frame flagship. 42.4 MP BSI full-frame sensor, 12 fps SLT burst with continuous AF, 5-axis IBIS, officially rated 500,000 actuations. ARW format; shutter count readable via encrypted MakerNote tag 0x9050. Explains SLT translucent-mirror design (fixed mirror = every shot increments shutter counter), and why a high count on this body is far less alarming than on entry-level cameras. Buying guidance for the final A-mount full-frame.
- [Sony Alpha A99 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a99-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony Alpha A99 (SLT-A99, 2012) — Sony's original full-frame A-mount SLT flagship. 24.3 MP full-frame Exmor sensor, 6 fps burst with continuous phase-detection AF via fixed translucent mirror, rated 200,000 actuations. ARW format; shutter count readable via MakerNote tag. Explains SLT technology (no mirror slap), impact of video recording on mechanical shutter count, A-mount lens compatibility, and buying guidance for this pioneering full-frame SLT.
- [Sony A68 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-a68-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony A68 (ILCA-68, 2016) — Sony's last consumer A-mount SLT camera. 24.2 MP APS-C Exmor sensor, 4D FOCUS with 425 phase-detect AF points covering ~92% of the frame, 8 fps burst, OLED EVF. Estimated shutter life ~100,000 actuations (not officially published). ARW format; shutter count readable via shuttercount.app or ExifTool (MakerNote tag 0x9050). Covers the SLT fixed-mirror design (every shot increments mechanical shutter), how to inspect the translucent mirror for dust and oil smears when buying used, A-mount lens compatibility, and comparison with the A77 II and E-mount alternatives.
- [Sony ZV-1 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-zv-1-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony ZV-1 (2020) — Sony's compact 1-inch vlog camera with a fixed ZEISS Vario-Sonnar T* 24–70mm f/1.8–2.8 lens, 20.1 MP Stacked BSI CMOS sensor, Real-Time Eye AF, and 4K HDR video. Estimated ~200,000-actuation shutter life (not officially published). Shutter count readable from ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050. Covers the electronic-shutter caveat (ZV-1 defaults to silent mode; mechanical count may be very low on video-heavy bodies), ZV-1 vs ZV-1 II differences, and used-camera buying guidance.
- [Sony RX100 VI Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-rx100-vi-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony RX100 VI (DSC-RX100M6, 2018) — the first RX100 with a 24–200mm 8.3× zoom lens (ZEISS Vario-Sonnar T* f/2.8–4.5), 20.1 MP 1-inch Exmor RS CMOS sensor, pop-up EVF, Fast Hybrid AF, 24 fps burst, and 4K video. Estimated ~200,000-actuation shutter life (not officially published). Shutter count readable from ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050. Covers silent-shutter caveat, pop-up EVF condition as a key used-camera check, RX100 VI vs VII differences (stacked sensor and AF upgrade on VII), and buying guidance for travel photographers.
- [Sony RX100 VII Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-rx100-vii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony RX100 VII (DSC-RX100M7, 2019) — Sony's flagship RX100 compact with a stacked 20.1 MP 1-inch sensor, 20 fps burst with real-time AF/AE tracking, and 4K HDR video. Covers the estimated ~200,000-actuation shutter life (Sony does not publish an official rating for compact cameras), how the count is stored encrypted in ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050, and what to inspect when buying this premium compact used.
- [Sony RX100 V Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-rx100-v-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony RX100 V (DSC-RX100M5, 2016) — the first RX100 with Fast Hybrid AF (315 phase-detection points covering 65% of the frame), carrying over the stacked 20.1 MP 1-inch Exmor RS CMOS sensor from the IV and the same ZEISS Vario-Sonnar T* 24–70 mm f/1.8–2.8 lens as the III, IV, and V. Estimated ~200,000-actuation shutter life (not officially published). Shutter count readable from ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050 (encrypted). Covers silent-shutter caveat, pop-up EVF condition as a key used-camera check, RX100 V vs VI comparison (AF upgrade vs lens reach trade-off), battery age considerations on 10-year-old bodies, and buying guidance for low-light photographers.
- [Sony RX100 III Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-rx100-iii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony RX100 III (DSC-RX100M3, 2014) — the first RX100 with a built-in pop-up OLED EVF and a redesigned ZEISS Vario-Sonnar T* 24–70 mm f/1.8–2.8 lens (replacing the II's 28–100 mm), plus a built-in 3-stop ND filter. Estimated ~200,000-actuation shutter life (not officially published). Shutter count readable from ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050. Does not have a silent/electronic shutter option — every frame increments the mechanical counter, making the count a reliable indicator of use. Covers RX100 III vs IV comparison (stacked sensor and 4K video on IV), ND filter operation, and buying guidance for this now 12-year-old compact.
- [Sony RX100 IV Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-rx100-iv-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony RX100 IV (DSC-RX100M4, 2015) — the first RX100 with a stacked Exmor RS CMOS sensor, enabling 4K UHD video at 30fps, super slow-motion at up to 40× (960fps capture), and a silent electronic shutter option. Estimated ~200,000-actuation mechanical shutter life (not officially published). Shutter count readable from ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050 (encrypted). Silent shutter caveat: frames shot in electronic mode do not increment the mechanical counter, so the reported count may understate total usage on heavy silent-mode users. Covers RX100 III vs IV differences (stacked sensor, 4K, silent shutter), RX100 IV vs V comparison (V adds phase-detect AF), 4K 5-minute clip limit, and buying guidance for this 10-year-old compact.
- [Sony RX100 II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-rx100-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony RX100 II (DSC-RX100M2, 2013) — the first RX100 with a back-illuminated (BSI) Exmor CMOS sensor for improved high-ISO performance, a Multi Interface Shoe enabling the optional FDA-EV1MK electronic viewfinder, NFC connectivity, and a 180°-tilting 3-inch LCD. Estimated ~200,000-actuation shutter life (not officially published). Shutter count readable from ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050. No silent shutter — every still photo increments the mechanical counter. Covers RX100 I vs II differences (BSI sensor, tilting LCD, hot shoe), FDA-EV1MK EVF accessory, and buying guidance for this 12-year-old compact.
- [Sony RX100 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-rx100-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the original Sony RX100 (DSC-RX100, 2012) — the camera that established the 1-inch sensor compact category. 20.2 MP BSI Exmor CMOS, ZEISS Vario-Sonnar T* 28–100 mm f/1.8–4.9 lens, no EVF, no tilting LCD. Estimated ~200,000-actuation shutter life (not officially published). Shutter count readable from ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050. No silent shutter — every frame increments the mechanical counter, making the count a complete and reliable record of use. Covers RX100 I vs II differences, the NP-BX1 battery ecosystem, lens fungus risk on 13-year-old bodies, and used-camera buying guidance.
- [Sony RX10 IV Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-rx10-iv-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony RX10 IV (DSC-RX10M4, 2017) — Sony's flagship super-zoom with a 24–600 mm Zeiss lens, stacked 20.1 MP 1-inch sensor, 24 fps burst with phase-detection AF, 4K HDR video, and built-in ND filter. Covers the estimated ~200,000-actuation shutter life, ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050 encryption, burst-shooting impact on shutter wear, and what to inspect on used wildlife/sports bodies.
- [Sony RX10 III Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-rx10-iii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony RX10 III (DSC-RX10M3, 2016) — Sony's super-zoom with a Zeiss Vario-Sonnar T* 8.8–220mm f/2.4–4.0 lens (24–600mm equiv), 20.1 MP 1-inch stacked CMOS sensor, 14 fps burst, and 4K video. Estimated shutter life ~200,000 actuations. Shutter count readable from ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050. Covers burst-shooting wear patterns, RX10 III vs RX10 IV comparison (phase-detection AF is the key upgrade), and used-camera buying guidance.
- [Sony RX10 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-rx10-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony RX10 (DSC-RX10, Mark I, 2013) — Sony's original large-sensor bridge camera with a 20.1 MP 1-inch BSI CMOS sensor and a constant f/2.8 Zeiss Vario-Sonnar T* 24–200 mm lens. Estimated shutter life ~200,000 actuations (not officially published). ARW format; shutter count readable via encrypted MakerNote tag 0x9050. Covers the constant f/2.8 aperture advantage for events and concerts, built-in ND filter operation, 1080/60p video (no 4K), contrast-detect-only AF, and buying guidance for this 12+ year old super-zoom.
- [Sony RX10 II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-rx10-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony RX10 II (DSC-RX10M2, 2015) — the first RX10 with a stacked BSI CMOS sensor, enabling 4K/30p video, 960/480/240 fps super slow-motion in 1080p, and 16 fps burst. Same constant f/2.8 Zeiss 24–200 mm lens as the Mark I. Estimated shutter life ~200,000 actuations (not officially published). ARW format; shutter count readable via encrypted MakerNote tag 0x9050. Covers the slow-motion video caveat (electronic shutter only, no mechanical wear), why video-heavy bodies often show very low mechanical counts, and used-camera buying guidance.
- [Sony NEX-6 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-nex-6-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony NEX-6 (2012) — Sony's mid-range NEX mirrorless with a 16.1 MP APS-C sensor, built-in OLED EVF, hybrid AF (25 PDAF + 99 CDAF points), and Wi-Fi. Estimated shutter life ~100,000 actuations (not officially published). Shutter count readable from ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050 (same encrypted structure as all Sony Alpha cameras). Covers how to check via shuttercount.app, JPEG caveat (encrypted MakerNote not present), used-camera buying ranges for a 2012 body, and comparison with the A6000 as the direct successor model.
- [Sony NEX-7 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-nex-7-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony NEX-7 (2011) — Sony's enthusiast APS-C mirrorless flagship at launch, featuring a class-leading 24.3 MP APS-C Exmor sensor, triple-dial control layout, built-in OLED EVF, and E-mount. Estimated shutter life ~100,000 actuations (not officially published). Shutter count readable from ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050. Covers how to check via shuttercount.app, JPEG caveat, buying guidance for this 14-year-old body, and comparison with the A6000/A6700 successors.
- [Sony NEX-5 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/sony-nex-5-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Sony NEX-5 (2010) — one of the world's first APS-C compact system cameras and the launch model of the Sony E-mount ecosystem. 14.2 MP Exmor APS-C CMOS, interchangeable E-mount lenses. Estimated shutter life ~100,000 actuations (not officially published). Shutter count readable from ARW MakerNote tag 0x9050 (same encrypted structure as all Sony Alpha and NEX cameras). Covers all four NEX-5 variants (NEX-5, NEX-5N, NEX-5R, NEX-5T) and their differences, E-mount backward compatibility with modern Sony bodies, used-camera buying guidance for this 15+ year old pioneer mirrorless.

## Fujifilm Camera Shutter Count Pages

- [Fujifilm GFX 100RF Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/fujifilm-gfx-100rf-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Fujifilm GFX 100RF (2025) — the 102 MP medium format compact fixed-lens camera. Covers the ~150,000-actuation estimated shutter life, RAF MakerNote ImageCount tag 0x1438, the card-format counter reset caveat, electronic shutter option (no mechanical wear), and comparison with GFX 100S II for buyers choosing between fixed-lens and interchangeable-lens medium format.
- [Fujifilm GFX 100S II Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/fujifilm-gfx-100s-ii-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Fujifilm GFX 100S II medium format mirrorless camera.
- [Fujifilm GFX 50R Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/fujifilm-gfx50r-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Fujifilm GFX 50R (2018) — the rangefinder-style medium format mirrorless sharing the 51.4 MP sensor with the GFX 50S in a more compact horizontal body. Estimated shutter life ~150,000 actuations (not officially published). RAF MakerNote ImageCount tag 0x1438; card-format resets the in-file counter. Covers the GFX 50R vs GFX 50S body differences and used-camera buying guidance.
- [Fujifilm X-E4 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/fujifilm-x-e4-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Fujifilm X-E4 rangefinder-style APS-C mirrorless camera.
- [Fujifilm X-H2 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/fujifilm-x-h2-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Fujifilm X-H2 high-resolution APS-C mirrorless camera.
- [Fujifilm X-H2S Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/fujifilm-x-h2s-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Fujifilm X-H2S stacked-sensor sports APS-C camera.
- [Fujifilm X-S10 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/fujifilm-x-s10-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Fujifilm X-S10 compact APS-C mirrorless camera.

- [Fujifilm X-T3 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/fujifilm-x-t3-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Fujifilm X-T3 enthusiast APS-C mirrorless camera.

- [Fujifilm X-T5 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/fujifilm-x-t5-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Fujifilm X-T5 40 MP APS-C mirrorless camera.
- [Fujifilm X-T50 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/fujifilm-x-t50-shutter-count/): Shutter count information for the Fujifilm X-T50.
- [Fujifilm X100VI Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/fujifilm-x100vi-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Fujifilm X100VI compact fixed-lens camera.
- [Fujifilm X-E1 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/fujifilm-x-e1-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Fujifilm X-E1 (2012) — the first X-E rangefinder-style camera. Covers the estimated ~150,000-actuation shutter life (Fujifilm doesn't publish official ratings), RAF MakerNote ImageCount tag 0x1438, the format-reset caveat, and what to look for when buying this vintage APS-C X-Trans I camera used.
- [Fujifilm X-M1 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/fujifilm-x-m1-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Fujifilm X-M1 (2013) — Fujifilm's most compact and affordable X-series camera at launch, sharing the 16.3 MP X-Trans CMOS I sensor with the X-Pro1. Estimated shutter life ~50,000 actuations (Fujifilm does not publish an official rating for entry-level bodies). Covers the RAF MakerNote ImageCount tag 0x1438, no viewfinder design, absence of electronic shutter, XF/XC lens compatibility, and buying guidance for this budget entry into the X system.

- [Fujifilm X-E3 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/fujifilm-x-e3-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Fujifilm X-E3 compact rangefinder-style mirrorless. Rated lifespan: 100,000 actuations.

- [Fujifilm X-Pro2 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/fujifilm-x-pro2-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Fujifilm X-Pro2. Rated lifespan: approximately 150,000 actuations. RAF MakerNote ImageCount tag 0x1438.
- [Fujifilm X-Pro3 Shutter Count](https://shuttercount.app/fujifilm-x-pro3-shutter-count/): Shutter count guide for the Fujifilm X-Pro3 rangefinder-style flagship. Rated lifespan: 300,000 actuations.
